activities_projects.title: Identifying Poverty Indicators
activities_projects.objective: Understand the multi-dimensional nature of poverty
steps: Observe your surroundings and list 5 signs of poverty (e.g., lack of shelter, malnutrition)
Categorize these signs into 'Economic' (no money) and 'Social' (no school/healthcare)
Identify which sign is the most common in your local area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that poverty is more than just low income
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance of examples and correct categorization (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Poverty Line Concept
activities_projects.objective: Learn how poverty is measured using the 'Poverty Line'
steps: Define the 'Poverty Line' based on the textbook
Note the minimum calorie requirement for Rural (2400) and Urban (2100) areas
Explain why rural people need more calories than urban people
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the calorie-based definition of the poverty line
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definition and reasoning for calorie differences (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Social Exclusion and Vulnerability
activities_projects.objective: Understand who is most likely to be poor
steps: Create a list of 'Vulnerable Groups' (e.g., Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Casual Labourers)
Define 'Social Exclusion' in your own words
Discuss why a widow or a physically challenged person might be more vulnerable to poverty
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies social groups most affected by poverty
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear identification of groups and accurate definitions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rural vs. Urban Poverty: A Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Compare the living conditions of the poor in different settings
steps: Read the stories of Ram Saran (Urban) and Lakha Singh (Rural) from the text
Make a table comparing their family size, source of income, and housing
Identify one common problem faced by both families
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between rural and urban poverty contexts
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate comparison table and logical conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inter-State Disparities Map
activities_projects.objective: Observe how poverty varies across different Indian states
steps: Identify the two poorest states (Odisha and Bihar) from the textbook data
Color them Red on the map
Color two states with low poverty (Punjab and Kerala) in Green
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the uneven distribution of poverty in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and coloring of states (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Causes of Poverty Checklist
activities_projects.objective: Identify the historical and economic reasons for poverty
steps: List three historical causes of poverty (e.g., British colonial rule)
List three modern causes (e.g., high population growth, lack of jobs)
Identify which cause you think is the hardest to solve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Categorizes the various factors leading to poverty
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical listing of historical and modern causes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anti-Poverty Schemes (MGNREGA)
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the government's flagship employment scheme
steps: Write the full form of MGNREGA
List two main features of this act (e.g., 100 days of guaranteed work)
Explain how this scheme helps a rural family during a drought
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the role of MGNREGA in poverty reduction
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct facts and explanation of the scheme's benefit (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Poverty Trends
activities_projects.objective: See the bigger picture of poverty worldwide
steps: Identify which country has seen the fastest decline in poverty (China)
Note the World Bank's definition of poverty (living on less than $1.90/day)
Mention one region where poverty is still very high (Sub-Saharan Africa)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates Indian poverty to global standards and trends
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of global data points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Poorest of the Poor'
activities_projects.objective: Understand inequalities within a poor family
steps: Explain why women, elderly people, and female infants are called 'poorest of the poor'
Describe how resources are distributed unequally even within a poor household
Suggest one way to help these specific members
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes gender and age-based inequalities in poverty
activities_projects.evaluation: Sensitivity and clarity in explaining internal family inequality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Future Challenges: Beyond Income
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on the expanding definition of poverty
steps: Define 'Human Poverty' as described in the chapter's conclusion
List three things besides food that a person needs for a dignified life (e.g., dignity, freedom from casteism)
Write a slogan for a 'Poverty-Free India'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects economic poverty to broader human rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Thoughtful definition and creative slogan (10 marks)
